In [1]:
# Importing necessary libraries
import pandas as pd
import seaborn as sns
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
from sklearn.datasets import load_iris
from sklearn.preprocessing import StandardScaler
from sklearn.cluster import KMeans, AgglomerativeClustering, DBSCAN
from sklearn.metrics import silhouette_score

# Load the Iris dataset directly from GitHub
url = 'https://raw.githubusercontent.com/uiuc-cse/data-fa14/gh-pages/data/iris.csv'
iris_df = pd.read_csv(url)

# Data Description
print("Data Description:")
print(iris_df.info())
print(iris_df.describe())

In [3]:
# Standardizing the features
scaler = StandardScaler()
X = scaler.fit_transform(iris_df.drop('species', axis=1))  # Ensure the column name is correct

# Model Training
kmeans = KMeans(n_clusters=3, random_state=42)
kmeans_labels = kmeans.fit_predict(X)
iris_df['KMeans_Cluster'] = kmeans_labels

hierarchical = AgglomerativeClustering(n_clusters=3)
hierarchical_labels = hierarchical.fit_predict(X)
iris_df['Hierarchical_Cluster'] = hierarchical_labels

dbscan = DBSCAN(eps=0.5, min_samples=5)
dbscan_labels = dbscan.fit_predict(X)
iris_df['DBSCAN_Cluster'] = dbscan_labels

C:\Users\dharm\anaconda3\Lib\site-packages\sklearn\cluster\_kmeans.py:1412: FutureWarning: The default value of `n_init` will change from 10 to 'auto' in 1.4. Set the value of `n_init` explicitly to suppress the warning
  super()._check_params_vs_input(X, default_n_init=10)

  warnings.warn(
In [4]:
# Graphs of Feature Distributions
plt.figure(figsize=(10, 6))
sns.histplot(iris_df['sepal_length'], kde=True)
plt.title('Sepal Length Distribution')
plt.xlabel('Sepal Length (cm)')
plt.ylabel('Frequency')
plt.show()
In [5]:

sns.histplot(iris_df['sepal_width'], kde=True)
plt.title('Sepal Width Distribution')
plt.xlabel('Sepal Width (cm)')
plt.ylabel('Frequency')
plt.show()

plt.ylabel('Frequency')

plt.xlabel('Petal Width (cm)')
plt.ylabel('Frequency')
plt.show()
In [8]:
# Silhouette scores
kmeans_silhouette = silhouette_score(X, kmeans_labels)
hierarchical_silhouette = silhouette_score(X, hierarchical_labels)
dbscan_silhouette = silhouette_score(X, dbscan_labels)

print("\nSilhouette Scores:")
print("K-means Clustering:", kmeans_silhouette)
print("Hierarchical Clustering:", hierarchical_silhouette)
print("DBSCAN Clustering:", dbscan_silhouette)

Silhouette Scores:
K-means Clustering: 0.5012342008377098
Hierarchical Clustering: 0.499447819212006
DBSCAN Clustering: 0.4462941405641163
In [9]:
# Cluster Visualization
plt.figure(figsize=(12, 6))
sns.scatterplot(x='sepal_length', y='sepal_width', hue='KMeans_Cluster', data=iris_df, palette='viridis')
plt.title('K-means Clustering')
plt.xlabel('Sepal Length (cm)')
plt.ylabel('Sepal Width (cm)')
plt.show()
In [10]:

sns.scatterplot(x='sepal_length', y='sepal_width', hue='Hierarchical_Cluster', data=iris_df, palette='viridis')
plt.title('Hierarchical Clustering')
plt.xlabel('Sepal Length (cm)')
plt.ylabel('Sepal Width (cm)')
plt.show()

plt.title('DBSCAN Clustering')

plt.ylabel('Sepal Width (cm)')
plt.show()
In [14]:
# Additional Visualization
plt.figure(figsize=(18, 6))

plt.subplot(1, 3, 1)
sns.scatterplot(x='sepal_length', y='sepal_width', hue='KMeans_Cluster', data=iris_df, palette='viridis')
plt.title('K-means Clustering')

plt.subplot(1, 3, 2)
sns.scatterplot(x='sepal_length', y='sepal_width', hue='Hierarchical_Cluster', data=iris_df, palette='viridis')
plt.title('Hierarchical Clustering')

plt.subplot(1, 3, 3)
sns.scatterplot(x='sepal_length', y='sepal_width', hue='DBSCAN_Cluster', data=iris_df, palette='viridis')
plt.title('DBSCAN Clustering')

plt.show()
